documentation.value:
Returns a lazy sequence of applying `f` to each element of `coll`.
```clojure
(map inc [1 2 3])       ;=> (2 3 4)
(map + [1 2] [3 4])     ;=> (4 6)
(map str [1 2 3])       ;=> ("1" "2" "3")
```
A function to apply to each element.
One or more collections. If multiple, `f` receives one element from each.

Returns a lazy sequence of items in `coll` for which `pred` returns truthy.
```clojure
(filter even? [1 2 3 4 5]) ;=> (2 4)
(filter #(> % 3) [1 5 2 4]) ;=> (5 4)
```
A predicate function that returns truthy/falsey.
The collection to filter.

Reduces a collection to a single value using `f`.
```clojure
(reduce + [1 2 3 4])       ;=> 10
(reduce + 0 [1 2 3 4])     ;=> 10
(reduce conj [] '(1 2 3))  ;=> [1 2 3]
```
A reducing function `(fn [acc val] ...)` of two arguments.
Optional initial value. If not supplied, first element of `coll` is used.
The collection to reduce.

Prints arguments separated by spaces followed by a newline to *out*.
```clojure
(println "Hello" "World") ; prints: Hello World
(println {:a 1})           ; prints: {:a 1}
```
Zero or more values to print.

Concatenates the string representations of its arguments.
```clojure
(str "Hello" " " "World")  ;=> "Hello World"
(str 1 2 3)                ;=> "123"
(str nil)                  ;=> ""
```
Zero or more values. `nil` becomes empty string.

Adds elements to a collection. Position depends on collection type (end for vectors, front for lists).
```clojure
(conj [1 2] 3)       ;=> [1 2 3]
(conj '(1 2) 3)      ;=> (3 1 2)
(conj #{1 2} 3)      ;=> #{1 2 3}
```
The collection to add to. Can be vector, list, set, or map.
One or more elements to add.

Associates key-value pairs with a map or vector.
```clojure
(assoc {} :a 1 :b 2)      ;=> {:a 1, :b 2}
(assoc [1 2 3] 1 :two)    ;=> [1 :two 3]
```
A map or vector.
The key (or index for vectors).
The value to associate.
Additional key-value pairs.

Returns the value for `key` in `map`, or `not-found`/nil.
```clojure
(get {:a 1 :b 2} :a)        ;=> 1
(get {:a 1} :c :default)    ;=> :default
```
A map, vector, set, or string.
The key to look up.
Optional value to return if key is not found (default `nil`).

Applies function `f` to the argument list formed by prepending `args` to `more`.
```clojure
(apply + [1 2 3])        ;=> 6
(apply str ["a" "b" "c"]) ;=> "abc"
(apply max 1 2 [3 4])   ;=> 4
```
The function to apply.
Arguments, with the last being a sequence.

Returns a function that is `f` with some arguments pre-filled.
```clojure
(def add5 (partial + 5))
(add5 3)   ;=> 8
(add5 10)  ;=> 15
```
The function to partially apply.
Arguments to pre-fill.

Returns a function that is the composition of `fns` (right to left).
```clojure
((comp str inc) 1)           ;=> "2"
((comp clojure.string/upper-case str) 42) ;=> "42"
```
Functions to compose, applied right to left.

Returns the first element of a collection, or `nil` if empty.
```clojure
(first [1 2 3])  ;=> 1
(first '())      ;=> nil
```
A collection or sequence.

Returns a sequence of all elements except the first. Returns `()` if empty.
```clojure
(rest [1 2 3])  ;=> (2 3)
(rest [1])      ;=> ()
```
A collection or sequence.

Returns a new sequence with `x` as the first element and `seq` as the rest.
```clojure
(cons 0 [1 2 3])  ;=> (0 1 2 3)
(cons :a nil)     ;=> (:a)
```
The element to prepend.
The rest of the sequence.
The target collection.
The source collection whose items are added.
